summary.intent: Fix four P2 bugs in the DateRangePicker component identified by a Codex review, using TDD (red-green cycle) and then sync the spec documentation.
summary.outcome: All four bugs were fixed and verified: disable-while-open reconciles to Closed (C1), SelectRangeComplete accepts range in any state (C2), and clear/preset triggers are disabled when readonly (C3/C4). 86 tests pass, clippy clean, coverage maintained at ~99.72%, and the spec was updated to match.

repo: DateRangePicker spec lives at spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md and must be updated alongside code changes — there are spec gates in CI that validate consistency.
The project enforces a coverage gate (~99.72% line coverage) and runs proptest + snapshot tests as separate verification steps beyond unit tests.
SelectPreset already handled the 'accept in any state, close only if Open' pattern — C2 fix mirrored that existing convention rather than inventing a new one.

The disabled guard in SyncProps was blocking Close/Escape/FocusOut events, so disabling while open would leave the picker stuck — reconciliation to Closed must happen before the disabled guard activates.

code.finding: SyncProps event handler needed to reconcile state to Closed when disabled=true and state=Open, before the disabled guard runs.
SelectRangeComplete was gated on Open state; fix makes it accept the range in any state and only close if was Open — matching SelectPreset's pattern.
Clear and preset trigger disabled conditions only checked `disabled`; `readonly` needed to be added as a second disabling condition.
New tests were placed near thematically related existing tests (disabled tests for C1, readonly-block tests for C2, clear/preset ARIA tests for C3/C4) — file is organized by behavior category.

workflow: TDD red-green cycle was enforced: all 4 tests were confirmed failing before any fix was applied, then fixed one-by-one.
Codex review ran as a background task and produced a structured verdict with unresolved thread IDs that could be fetched separately.
Spec synchronization is a mandatory step after behavior changes — anatomy rows, transition tables, and trigger doc comments all needed updating.
Coverage baseline was captured before writing tests to confirm no regression after fixes.

friction: Locating the correct insertion points in tests.rs required multiple Read + Bash grep passes before each Edit — the test file is large enough that blind insertion would have misplaced tests.
The C2 fix required multiple Read/Edit iterations on mod.rs to get the state-conditional close logic correct.
